name: create_file
description:
Create a new plain-text file at the specified path. In restricted mode paths must resolve inside the current registered source scope; allow-all/non-restricted permission modes can use all registered sources, and open mode can also use absolute local paths outside registered sources. Parent directories are created automatically. Supports text-based formats (md, txt, json, csv, rs, py, etc.). Paths may be absolute or relative to a source root.

Use this tool when:
- You need to create a brand-new text file
- The file does not yet exist (or you explicitly set overwrite to true)

Do not use Python or run_shell snippets for ordinary plain-text file creation. Do not use this tool for DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/PDF files. Use run_shell + doc-script-editor (Python Office libraries) for Office/PDF creation, validation, conversion, rendering, existing-file edits, extraction, redaction, formula checks, OOXML surgery, or template-preserving work.
For editing existing text files, use edit_file instead.
For notes in the notes/ subdirectory, use write_note instead.

path.description: Path to the file to create (absolute or relative to a source directory; open mode also accepts absolute local paths outside registered sources).

content.description: The text content to write to the file.

overwrite.description: If true, overwrite the file if it already exists. Default: false.
